name: Reality denial
description: A clear event is flatly denied to make you distrust your own observation.
response: I remember it differently. I am not debating my memory with you.
exit: End the loop and write down the observable facts.

name: Memory attack
description: The focus moves from what happened to whether you are capable of remembering anything.
response: We can discuss the event without insulting my ability to remember.
exit: Return to dates, messages, witnesses, or pause the conversation.

name: Minimization
description: Harmful behavior is reframed as too small for you to mention.
response: It affected me, and I am asking you not to repeat it.
exit: State the consequence once, then follow through.

name: Blame reversal
description: Their behavior disappears while your reaction becomes the alleged problem.
markers: look what you made me do
response: You are responsible for your behavior. I am responsible for mine.
exit: Refuse the false assignment and leave if escalation continues.

name: DARVO pattern
description: Deny, attack, then reverse victim and offender when accountability appears.
response: We were discussing one specific behavior. I will not switch roles or topics.
exit: Use a broken-record response and stop after one repetition.

name: Social isolation pressure
description: Other relationships are discredited so one person controls your source of reality.
markers: they are all lying to you
response: I decide who I speak with. Secrecy is not a condition I accept.
exit: Contact a trusted person privately and review your safety.

name: Moving goalposts
description: Success is repeatedly redefined so you can never resolve the accusation.
response: We agreed on the standard already. I am not accepting a new one after the fact.
exit: Put agreements in writing or stop participating.

name: Tone policing
description: Your delivery is used to avoid the substance of what you said.
response: We can discuss my tone after we address the behavior I named.
exit: Repeat the topic once, then pause.

name: False consensus
description: Unnamed people are invoked to make you feel isolated or defective.
response: Name the specific person and claim, or leave 'everyone' out of it.
exit: Do not defend yourself against an invisible jury.

name: Threat disguised as concern
description: Control is presented as protection, care, or advice.
markers: i am only trying to help
response: Advice is optional. Pressure and consequences are not care.
exit: Make the decision away from the person applying pressure.

name: Moving the goalposts
description: The standard changes after you meet it, making success permanently unreachable.
response: We agreed on the original standard. I will not accept a new one retroactively.
exit: Restate the written agreement and stop negotiating against a shifting target.

name: False urgency
description: Pressure is created so you decide before you can check facts or ask for support.
response: I do not make pressured decisions. I will answer after I have time to review this.
exit: Pause, verify independently, and let artificial deadlines expire.

name: Forced team choice
description: Disagreement is reframed as betrayal or proof that you support an enemy.
markers: if you were really with me
response: Disagreeing with you is not betrayal. I can care and still say no.
exit: Refuse the false binary and speak to a neutral person.

name: Punishing silence
description: Contact or affection is withdrawn to force compliance rather than create a healthy pause.
markers: you will not hear from me
response: You may take space. I will not trade compliance for communication.
exit: Stop chasing and decide what respectful reconnection would require.

name: Public-private switch
description: Someone acts kind around witnesses but cruel in private, making disclosure harder.
markers: it is your word against mine
response: Public charm does not erase private behavior. I am documenting what happens.
exit: Keep factual records and involve a trusted third party when safe.

name: Debt manufacturing
description: Unrequested favors are treated as a contract that gives someone control over you.
response: I appreciate help, but it did not purchase control over my choices.
exit: Decline future strings-attached favors and clarify agreements beforehand.

name: Information flooding
description: A simple issue is buried under rapid claims so you cannot answer or verify any one point.
response: Choose one specific claim. We will address one thing at a time.
exit: Ask for claims in writing or end the conversation if the flood continues.

name: Boundary bargaining
description: A clear no is treated as the beginning of a negotiation rather than an answer.
markers: what would make you say yes
response: No is my answer. I am not negotiating this boundary.
exit: Repeat once, then disengage or physically leave when safe.

name: Weaponized vulnerability
description: Personal information shared in trust is later used to shame, threaten, or discredit you.
markers: this is why you have issues
response: I shared that in confidence. Using it against me ends this conversation.
exit: Reduce access, preserve threats, and seek trusted support.

name: Proxy pressure
description: Unnamed people are invoked to create social pressure without verifiable feedback.
response: Name the specific person and observable concern, or leave others out of this.
exit: Do not defend yourself against an invisible audience.
